title: Logger fields should be private static readonly
description: Regardless of the logging framework in use (Microsoft.Extension.Logging, Serilog, Log4net, NLog, …), logger fields should be: private: this restricts access to the logger from outside the enclosing type (class, struct, record…). Using any other access modifier would allow other types to use the logger to log messages in the type where it’s defined. static: making the logger field static will ensure that the lifetime of the object doesn’t depend on the lifetime of the instance of the enclosing type.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

triggering_conditions: ILogger field not marked as private
ILogger field not marked as static
ILogger field not marked as readonly
exclusion_conditions: ILogger field with private, static, and readonly modifiers
checks.reasoning: Logger fields should be private (restrict access), static (shared across instances), and readonly (prevent reassignment)
